summary_one_line: Input must drive the internal-subset scanner into state==']' (a DOCTYPE ']'-then-close), reached only via value_profile comparison-gradient feedback.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DOCTYPE internal-subset body whose unescaped scan reaches a ']' character (setting the scanner state machine to state==']') and then a following '>' / blank, i.e. a `]` closing sequence inside the parsed DTD section. Reaching the BLOCKER true side at parser.c:11261 requires the byte stream to advance the state machine through '<' / quote / ']' transitions until state holds ']'.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile_cmplog winner resolves 9/10 and actually enters state==']' (W shows T=4 at L11261 and downstream W-only execution of the `if (*cur == '>')`, `IS_BLANK_CH`, and state==' ' arms at 11262-11286). Its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that incrementally match the multi-byte DTD structure, so the scanner state machine is steered into the ']' state.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser blocks 8/10 and never sets state==']' — L shows T=0 at L11261 and never executes the 11262-11286 W-only arms; its scan stalls in the generic state-tracking arms (state!=0, '<', quote) without reaching the ']' close. Lacking CMP_MAP gradient, it gets no partial-progress credit for assembling the ']'-bearing internal subset.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets: each intercepted comparison contributes Hamming/prefix-distance feedback so inputs that partially match the multi-byte DTD ']'-close structure are retained and refined. cmplog (I2S only, no CMP_MAP) gets no such gradient credit, so it cannot incrementally walk the scanner state machine into state==']'. The delta between the two involved fuzzers is exactly value_profile, so the winning mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient.

evidence_trail.claim: The single decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating cmplog, and the technique delta between them is value_profile.
The winner resolves 9/10 while the loser blocks 8/10 at this branch.
The blocker is the state==']' arm of the internal-subset scanner state machine.
The scanner state is set to ']' when the current character is a quote or a ']' literal.
The winner actually enters state==']' (T=4) at the blocker while the loser never does (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L11261  T=4 F=397  T=0 F=166  else if (state == ']') {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Only the winner executes the downstream ']'-state arms (the '>' close and IS_BLANK_CH transitions).
value_profile_cmplog feedback includes CMP_MAP gradient buckets in addition to edge and CMP-buffer signals.
Both inputs carry a well-formed XML prolog and a DOCTYPE prefix, so the structural difference is downstream scanner progress, not the prolog.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile delta), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S lineage check does not apply. Both involved fuzzers (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) already carry I2SRandReplace, so I2S cannot be the discriminating technique; the only difference is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback. Lineage dash-row evidence would be uninformative here because it is present in both fuzzers and does not separate winner from loser.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose gate requires reaching the scanner state==']' shows no resolution-rate advantage for value_profile/value_profile_cmplog over cmplog/naive — i.e. CMP_MAP gradient feedback gives no measurable lift in reaching the ']'-close state.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from CMP_MAP gradient to incrementally building the multi-byte ']'-close is inferred from the state==']' coverage divergence; I have no direct seed-byte evidence of a gradient-driven refinement chain producing the ']'.
